_meta.description: Labeled seed corpus for the email follow-up detection quality eval (#1606 feature, #1950 tracking). Each case is ONE sent thread (a conversation the user started) hand-labeled with whether it genuinely 'awaits_reply' -- i.e. the user asked for something / expects a response that has not arrived. Scored by gaia.eval.followup_quality: the corpus thread is replayed into a FakeGmailBackend and the REAL detector (gaia_agent_email.tools.followup_tools.check_followups_impl) is run over it; whether the thread is flagged 'awaiting_reply' is the prediction, matched against 'awaits_reply'. Detection is deterministic (latest-message-outbound + age + external-recipient) -- this eval measures where that heuristic diverges from human judgment: FALSE POSITIVES (thanks/FYI/acknowledgment notes the user sent that need no reply but get flagged) and FALSE NEGATIVES (a genuine question whose latest inbound message is only an out-of-office auto-reply, which the detector mistakes for a real answer). To isolate the CONTENT/reply-state judgment from the detector's timing grace-window, every outbound-latest case is authored past the 3-day window (age_days >= window); the only thing that varies is the content and the inbound-reply state. HARD NEGATIVES (awaits_reply == false) are deliberate and first-class: a sent note that needs no reply must NOT be flagged, and any flag there is a false positive in the precision denominator. The aggregate precision / recall / F1 over the awaits_reply flag are compared to followups_gate_thresholds.json in report mode (enforce:false).

detection_params.note: The scorer runs check_followups_impl with window_days=3 and a fixed injected now, so ages are deterministic. A thread is flagged only when its LATEST message is the user's own outbound mail, at least window_days old, to at least one external recipient.

case_schema.scenario: str -- short label for what this sent thread is
case_schema.awaits_reply: bool -- the human label: does this thread genuinely await an inbound reply the user is waiting on?
case_schema.is_hard_negative: bool -- true when the thread does NOT await a reply (awaits_reply == false); must agree with awaits_reply
case_schema.thread: list[{direction: 'outbound'|'inbound', from?: str, to?: str, subject: str, body: str, age_days: number}] -- the conversation in chronological order (oldest first). Outbound = the user's mail (from defaults to the user, to is the recipient); inbound = a counterparty's reply (from is the counterparty, to defaults to the user). age_days is how many days ago the message was sent, relative to the fixed eval 'now'.

fu-001.scenario: genuine question -- confirm a meeting time, no reply

thread.subject: Design review time
thread.body:
Hi Dana,

Can we move Thursday's design review from 1pm to 3pm? Let me know if that works and I'll update the invite.

Sam

fu-002.scenario: document review request to external counsel, no reply

thread.subject: Licensing agreement -- redline
thread.body:
Dear Robert,

I've attached our redline of the licensing agreement. Could you review sections 4 and 7 and let me know if the revised indemnity language is acceptable?

Thanks,
Sam

fu-003.scenario: quote request to a vendor, no reply

thread.subject: Pricing for the enterprise tier
thread.body:
Hello,

We're evaluating your enterprise plan for ~200 seats. Could you send a formal quote including volume discount and annual billing options?

Best,
Sam

fu-004.scenario: approval request to a teammate, no reply

thread.subject: PR #812 ready for your approval
thread.body:
Hi Priya,

The migration PR is green and ready. Could you review and approve when you get a chance? I'd like to merge before the Friday freeze.

Sam

fu-005.scenario: open question needing an answer, no reply

thread.subject: Which commit shipped in the staging deploy?
thread.body:
Hey Marcus,

QA is chasing a regression on staging. Do you know which commit went out in this morning's deploy? I need it to bisect.

Sam

fu-006.scenario: false-negative trap -- genuine ask, latest inbound is only an out-of-office auto-reply

thread.subject: Intro call times
thread.body:
Hi Grace,

Great to connect. Are you free Tuesday 2pm or Thursday 11am for a 30-minute intro call? I'll send an invite once you confirm.

Sam

thread.subject: Automatic reply: Intro call times
thread.body:
I am out of the office until Monday with limited access to email. For urgent matters contact my assistant at ops@partnerco.com. I will respond when I return.

Grace

fu-neg-001.scenario: hard negative -- thank-you note the user sent, no reply needed

thread.subject: Thanks!
thread.body:
Hi Grace,

Thanks so much for the demo yesterday -- the team was really impressed. No need to reply, just wanted to pass along the kind words.

Sam

fu-neg-002.scenario: hard negative -- FYI status broadcast, explicitly no action needed

thread.subject: 2.4 deployed cleanly
thread.body:
Hi all,

Just letting you know the 2.4 release deployed to production this afternoon with no issues. Metrics look normal. No action needed -- sharing for visibility.

Sam

fu-neg-003.scenario: hard negative -- closing acknowledgment, conversation already settled

thread.subject: Design review time
thread.body:
3pm Thursday works great on my end -- see you then.

Dana

thread.subject: Re: Design review time
thread.body:
Sounds good, see you Thursday at 3pm. Thanks Dana!

Sam

fu-neg-004.scenario: hard negative -- shared an article for visibility, no ask

thread.subject: Interesting read on NPU scheduling
thread.body:
Hey Marcus,

Saw this and thought of our roadmap discussion -- sharing for visibility, no need to respond.

https://example.com/npu-scheduling

Sam

fu-neg-005.scenario: hard negative -- polite decline that closes the loop, no reply needed

thread.subject: Team offsite -- headcount
thread.body:
Hi all, please RSVP yes/no for the offsite by Friday.

Events

thread.subject: Re: Team offsite -- headcount
thread.body:
Thanks for organizing -- I won't be able to make it this time, no worries. Have fun!

Sam

fu-neg-006.scenario: hard negative -- thread answered by a substantive inbound reply

thread.subject: Which commit shipped in the staging deploy?
thread.body:
Hey Priya, do you know which commit went out in this morning's staging deploy?

Sam

thread.subject: Re: Which commit shipped in the staging deploy?
thread.body:
It was a1b2c3d -- the checkout refactor. That's almost certainly the regression; I'll revert it now.

Priya

fu-neg-007.scenario: hard negative -- approval granted, thread resolved by the reply

thread.subject: PTO approval
thread.body:
Hi Tom, could you approve my PTO request for August 4-8 in the HR system?

Sam

thread.subject: Re: PTO approval
thread.body:
Approved -- enjoy the break!

Tom

fu-neg-008.scenario: hard negative -- note-to-self, no external recipient can ever reply

thread.subject: Reminder: renew the domain
thread.body: Renew acme.io before it expires on the 22nd. Card ending 4471.

fu-neg-009.scenario: hard negative -- FYI broadcast that got a courtesy reply, no ask outstanding

thread.subject: Q2 metrics recap
thread.body:
Hi all, attaching the Q2 metrics recap for your awareness. No action needed.

Sam

thread.subject: Re: Q2 metrics recap
thread.body:
Thanks for putting this together, Sam -- really useful.

Elena

fu-neg-010.scenario: hard negative -- confirmation the user sent that ends the exchange

thread.subject: Invite: intro call Thursday 11am
thread.body:
Sending an invite for Thursday 11am -- let me know if that still works.

Grace

thread.subject: Re: Invite: intro call Thursday 11am
thread.body:
Confirmed, Thursday 11am works -- accepted the invite. Talk then!

Sam
